channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.06586v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained predominantly on English data encode substantial world knowledge, yet often fail to express it reliably in other languages, a phenomenon known as cross-lingual factual inconsistency. To study and address this, we i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, LLM, large language model, arxiv, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Re-Centering Humans in LLM Personalization
item.description: arXiv:2606.06614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite growing interest, most evaluations of large language models' (LLMs') personalization abilities have relied on synthetic data. It remains unclear how well current personalization systems work for real users. In this paper, we study the gap in L...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, study, LLM, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CAF-Gen: A Multi-Agent System for Enriching Argumentation Structures
item.description: arXiv:2606.06646v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formalizing complex reasoning from natural text is one of the central challenges in computational linguistics. It requires systems to understand not just keywords but also the context and complex reasoning embedded in a text. Current Argument Mining (...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, reasoning, arxiv, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Piggyback Hypothesis of Generalization: Explaining and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2606.06667v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The mechanisms behind LLMs' broad over-generalization beyond training examples remain unclear. Emergent misalignment (EM) offers a striking case study: finetuning on narrow tasks induces broad misalignment to semantically-unrelated test domains. In th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> template, RAG, study, LLM, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Do People Actually Want From AI? Mapping Preference Plurality
item.description: arXiv:2606.06674v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are often fine-tuned through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to align with people's preferences and values. However, this method has known limitations: it aggregates conflicting preferences, often relies ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, RLHF, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HKJudge: A Legal Discourse-Annotated Corpus for Interpreting What Courts Find, How They Reason, and What They Rule
item.description: arXiv:2606.06679v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Court judgments are central to legal practice and jurisprudence, yet discourse analysis of Hong Kong judgments has received limited attention, owing largely to the absence of expert-annotated corpora. We introduce the Hong Kong Judgment Discourse Data...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, zero-shot, analysis, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Elmes*: Automated Construction of Fine-Grained Evaluation Rubrics for Large Language Models in Long-Tail Educational Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2606.06546v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating large language models (LLMs) for education requires measuring how models teach, not only what they know. Existing benchmarks emphasize domain-general correctness or depend on manually designed rubrics that scale poorly to long-tail pedagogi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, LLM, large language model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Skip a Layer or Loop It? Learning Program-of-Layers in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.06574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) perform inference by following a fixed depth and order, non-recurrent execution of all layers. We reveal the wide existence of training-free, flexible, dynamic program-of-layers (PoLar), where pretrained layers can be pack...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, reasoning, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Data-Efficient Autoregressive-to-Diffusion Language Models via On-Policy Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2606.06712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the transformation of autoregressive models (ARLMs) into diffusion language models (DLMs). Rather than pretraining from scratch, prior work replaces the causal attention in ARLMs with bidirectional attention and then trains the resulting mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, attention, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Position: Don't Just "Fix it in Post": A Science of AI Must Study Training Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2606.06533v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What would it mean to have a scientific understanding of AI? Models are not static objects: they are snapshots of time-evolving processes shaped by data, objectives, architectures, and optimization dynamics. Yet much of AI research treats models as fi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, study, arxiv, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Study of Parallel Continuous Local Search
item.description: arXiv:2606.06656v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study parallel Continuous Local Search (CLS) as a solution approach for Boolean satisfiability problems with symmetric pseudo-Boolean (PB) constraints. Here, the $n$-variable PB-satisfiability problem is relaxed to a continuous optimisation problem...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, API, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are you sure? A Comprehensive and Comprehensible Survey of Uncertainty Quantification in Symbolic Regression
item.description: arXiv:2606.06567v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Symbolic regression (SR) is a class of methods that systematically explore the space of mathematical functions to discover models that accurately capture the underlying relationships in a dataset. Despite recent advances in the field, a lack of suppor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, research, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DiBS: Diffusion-Informed Branch Selection
item.description: arXiv:2606.06518v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sudoku is a representative constraint satisfaction problem that requires global structural reasoning under strict discrete constraints. The existing works of solving Sudoku mainly focus on two dominant approaches, i.e., traditional heuristic and deep ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Attack Selection in Agentic AI Control Evaluations Meaningfully Decreases Safety
item.description: arXiv:2606.06529v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: An attacker that strategically chooses when to attack is much harder to catch than one that attacks indiscriminately. AI control is a safety framework for deploying capable but untrusted AI agents under the oversight of a weaker, trusted monitor and a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Gaussian Process Latent Factor Regression for Low-Data, High-Dimensional Output Problems
item.description: arXiv:2606.06576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the sciences, regression tasks often require predicting high-dimensional outputs from few training examples. Multi-output Gaussian processes excel in low-data regimes but typically struggle with high-dimensional outputs. Compress-then-predict pipel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, analysis, arxiv, compression, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
